scenarios.title: Current Reg M-A meta report
scenarios.purpose: Measure the core happy path: a current-format Champions meta research pass that leaves a concrete artifact on disk.
successChecklist: The file uses the required section headings in the required order.
The content is Champions-first and based on current-source research rather than generic VGC commentary.
scenarios.prompt: Use the local Codex skill "vgc-meta-research". Research the current Pokemon Champions Regulation M-A metagame and write the result to docs/evals/plugin-eval/meta-report-reg-m-a.md. The output file must use these exact section headings in this order: Format Check, Meta Snapshot, Popular Teams and Cores, Strategic Trends, Anti-Meta Openings, Sources. Use live current sources, clearly separate sourced facts from inference, and make the report useful to a competitive player deciding what to prepare for right now.
scenarios.finalMessagePreview:
Wrote the report to [docs/evals/plugin-eval/meta-report-reg-m-a.md](/var/folders/v5/r_d_k431205bf3l7_cwmvcyc0000gn/T/plugin-eval-reg-m-a-meta-report-ZbqQtT/workspace/docs/evals/plugin-eval/meta-report-reg-m-a.md).

It uses the required section order, distinguishes sourced facts from inference, and is grounded in live sources checked on April 19, 2026:
- official Regulation Set M-A rules
- official Pokémon Champions transition context
- ChampionsMeta tournament tracking
- Champions Lab ladder + tournament meta data

I did not run tests since this was a documentation-only update.

scenarios.title: Single-mon meta fit research
scenarios.purpose: Measure whether the skill can answer a narrower competitive-research question without losing format accuracy or output structure.
successChecklist: The writeup stays narrowly focused on Hisuian Zoroark's meta fit rather than drifting into generic team-building.
The output explicitly labels thin-data or inference cases instead of overstating certainty.
scenarios.prompt: Use the local Codex skill "vgc-meta-research". Research how Hisuian Zoroark currently fits into Pokemon Champions Regulation M-A and write the result to docs/evals/plugin-eval/hisuian-zoroark-meta-fit.md. The output file must still use the exact section headings Format Check, Meta Snapshot, Popular Teams and Cores, Strategic Trends, Anti-Meta Openings, Sources, but interpret them through the lens of Hisuian Zoroark's place in the live meta. Distinguish sourced facts from inference and be explicit if public data is thin.
scenarios.finalMessagePreview:
Wrote the note to [docs/evals/plugin-eval/hisuian-zoroark-meta-fit.md](/var/folders/v5/r_d_k431205bf3l7_cwmvcyc0000gn/T/plugin-eval-mon-fit-lens-pVUSTA/workspace/docs/evals/plugin-eval/hisuian-zoroark-meta-fit.md).

It keeps the required headings, uses current live sources for Regulation M-A, and clearly labels the thin H-Zoroark-specific parts as inference.

scenarios.title: Format-conflict boundary handling
scenarios.purpose: Check whether the skill handles mixed-format or stale-mechanic requests honestly instead of confidently blending incompatible assumptions.
successChecklist: The output explicitly corrects the Tera/Champions mismatch instead of carrying the bad assumption forward.
The response shows boundary discipline when the request mixes incompatible formats.
scenarios.prompt: Use the local Codex skill "vgc-meta-research". A user asks for the current VGC meta but also insists Terastallization is active in Pokemon Champions Regulation M-A. Research the current format and write a response artifact to docs/evals/plugin-eval/format-conflict-boundary.md. The file must use the exact section headings Format Check, Meta Snapshot, Popular Teams and Cores, Strategic Trends, Anti-Meta Openings, Sources. Explicitly correct the mechanic mismatch, explain the boundary between confirmed current rules and community meta inference, and avoid pretending Scarlet/Violet format assumptions still apply.
scenarios.finalMessagePreview:
Wrote the artifact at [format-conflict-boundary.md](/var/folders/v5/r_d_k431205bf3l7_cwmvcyc0000gn/T/plugin-eval-format-conflict-boundary-AozmuH/workspace/docs/evals/plugin-eval/format-conflict-boundary.md).

It:
- uses the exact required section headings
- corrects the mechanic mismatch by treating Terastallization as unconfirmed for Regulation M-A
- separates official rules from community meta inference
- avoids carrying over Scarlet/Violet assumptions into Champions

No tests were run, since this was a docs-only update.
